phase_name: Start Recording Does Nothing — Root Cause Diagnosis and Fix
problem_statement: After a new build, running `yaptap` opens the app and the menu renders, but clicking 'Start Recording' has no visible effect: the icon does not change to the active state, no recording starts, and no error alert appears. The hotkey (⌥Space) is unaffected by this issue (it uses a separate rdev thread). Only the menu button path is broken.
root_cause_analysis: The event loop in run_app() drains TrayIconEvent BEFORE MenuEvent on each iteration (app.rs lines 641, 651). On macOS, when the user clicks the tray icon, the native NSMenu runs a modal event loop that blocks our nextEventMatchingMask: pump until the menu closes. When the user then clicks 'Start Recording', both a TrayIconEvent (the icon click) and a MenuEvent (the item click) queue up in their respective channels. On the next loop iteration: (1) TrayIconEvent fires first → build_menu() is called → a NEW menu is constructed with NEW MenuId values → menu_actions is replaced; (2) MenuEvent fires second → looks up the old MenuId → not found → nothing dispatched. AppEvent::HotkeyPressed is never sent so recording never starts.

issues.title: Process MenuEvent before TrayIconEvent in the event loop to prevent ID invalidation
issues.description: The event loop drains TrayIconEvent (which rebuilds the menu and replaces all MenuId values in menu_actions) before it drains MenuEvent. When both channels have events queued from the same user interaction (icon click → item click), the TrayIconEvent-triggered rebuild evicts the IDs from the displayed menu before MenuEvent is processed, causing every menu action to silently drop. Fix: move the MenuEvent drain loop to execute before the TrayIconEvent drain loop in run_app().
checks: In app.rs run_app() event loop, confirm the MenuEvent drain (`while let Ok(event) = tray_icon::menu::MenuEvent::receiver().try_recv()`) appears BEFORE the TrayIconEvent drain (`while let Ok(_event) = tray_icon::TrayIconEvent::receiver().try_recv()`).
Run `cargo build` and confirm exit 0.
Click 'Start Recording' in the menu — confirm the tray icon switches to the active (all-bars-equal) variant.
Click 'Stop Recording' in the menu — confirm the tray icon returns to idle.

issues.title: Add rebuild_menu() after AppState transitions so menu label reflects current recording state
issues.description: The menu label ('Start Recording' / 'Stop Recording' / 'Processing…') only updates when TrayIconEvent fires (icon clicked). If the user triggers recording via the hotkey, or the pipeline completes, the menu label is stale until the user re-opens the icon menu. The spec (menubar.md) shows the record toggle as reflecting current state. Fix: call rebuild_menu() immediately after each state transition: Idle→Recording, Recording→Processing, and PipelineDone (Processing→Idle). The TrayIconEvent-triggered rebuild is kept for prompt list refresh (menubar.md: 'prompt entries are refreshed each time the menu is opened').
checks: In the AppEvent::HotkeyPressed handler (Idle → Recording branch in app.rs), add a `rebuild_menu()` call after switch_icon() and after setting state to Recording.
In the AppEvent::HotkeyPressed handler (Recording → Processing branch), add a `rebuild_menu()` call after setting state to Processing.
In the AppEvent::PipelineDone handler, add a `rebuild_menu()` call after switch_icon() (the state is already set to Idle before switch_icon).
Run `cargo build` exit 0; run `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean.
Press ⌥Space to start recording → immediately open the menu → confirm label reads 'Stop Recording'.
Press ⌥Space to stop → wait for pipeline → open menu → confirm label reads 'Start Recording'.

issues.title: Add tracing::debug!/info! to event loop handlers to make dispatch order observable
issues.description: In app mode, all tracing output is invisible (no terminal). To diagnose event ordering issues and state transitions, add structured tracing calls at: TrayIconEvent receipt, MenuEvent receipt and dispatch, and AppEvent::HotkeyPressed state transition. This enables `RUST_LOG=debug ./target/debug/yaptap` to confirm the exact order events fire.
checks: Add `tracing::debug!("TrayIconEvent received")` at the top of the TrayIconEvent while-let body.
Add `tracing::debug!(menu_id = ?event.id, "MenuEvent received")` at the top of the MenuEvent while-let body.
Add `tracing::debug!(menu_id = ?event.id, found = menu_actions.contains_key(&event.id), "MenuEvent dispatch")` before the if-let-Some block in the MenuEvent handler.
Add `tracing::info!(state = ?state, "HotkeyPressed")` at the start of the AppEvent::HotkeyPressed handler.

Run `RUST_LOG=debug ./target/debug/yaptap`, click Start Recording; confirm the log shows MenuEvent received BEFORE TrayIconEvent received (after P6-I001 fix), dispatch found=true, and HotkeyPressed state=Idle.
Run `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean.

issues.title: start_recording() error alert gives no microphone-permission guidance and missing tracing::warn!
issues.description: macOS requires microphone permission (TCC). If denied, cpal's build_input_stream() may fail with a low-level CoreAudio error string that does not mention System Settings → Microphone. The current Err branch in app.rs only calls show_alert with the raw error. Fix: (1) add tracing::warn! before the alert, (2) when the error string contains a known permission indicator, prepend a hint pointing to System Settings → Privacy & Security → Microphone.
checks: Add `tracing::warn!(error = %e, "start_recording failed")` immediately before the show_alert call in the Err(e) branch of the start_recording() call.

Inspect e.to_string() for known permission substrings ('561015905', 'not permitted', 'kAudioHardwareNotRunningError', '-536870174'). If matched, show: 'Microphone access is required. Grant it in System Settings → Privacy & Security → Microphone, then restart YapTap.' Otherwise keep the existing 'Failed to start recording: {e}' message.
Run `cargo build` exit 0; run `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean.
Revoke microphone permission in System Settings → Privacy & Security → Microphone for the yaptap binary → click Start Recording → confirm a helpful alert appears.
Re-grant permission → confirm recording starts normally.

issues.title: Document RUST_LOG=debug launch command for app-mode debugging in AGENT.md
issues.description: In app mode, stderr is invisible when launched from Finder. To observe tracing output during testing, the binary must be launched from a terminal with RUST_LOG set. AGENT.md has no debugging section. Add the command so future sessions know how to diagnose app-mode issues.
checks: Add a '## Debugging' section to AGENT.md containing: `RUST_LOG=debug ./target/debug/yaptap` — launches app mode with all tracing output visible in the terminal.
Confirm AGENT.md has no status-report content (per project rules).
Commit the change.

issues.title: Audio device error callback fires spuriously after recording has already stopped
issues.description: The on_error closure registered with start_recording() lives for the lifetime of the cpal Stream. If the audio device disconnects while the pipeline thread is calling stop_and_save() (which drops the stream), the closure races and sends AppEvent::PipelineDone(Err(...)) after AppState has already returned to Idle, producing a spurious 'audio device error' alert. Fix: guard the closure with an Arc<AtomicBool> that is cleared when recording stops.
checks: Add `let recording_active = Arc::new(AtomicBool::new(false));` in the Idle→Recording branch of the HotkeyPressed handler before calling start_recording().
Set `recording_active.store(true, Ordering::SeqCst)` after Ok(handle) is returned.
Clone the Arc into the on_error closure; inside the closure, only send PipelineDone(Err) if `recording_active.load(Ordering::SeqCst)` is true.

In the Recording→Processing branch, after spawning the pipeline thread, store a clone of the Arc in shared state (or pass it to the pipeline thread) and set it to false when run_pipeline_inner() takes the AudioHandle.
Run `cargo build` exit 0; run `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean; run `cargo test` — 10/10 pass.

issues.title: Script path resolution missing middle fallback — fails for installed binary with non-project cwd
issues.description: transcribe_script_path() and llm_script_path() try (1) `<binary_parent>/../../src/core/<script>` then fall back to (2) literal `src/core/<script>` (cwd-relative). Option (1) works for target/debug/ and target/release/ dev builds. Option (2) requires cwd == project root, which is not the case when launching from Finder or a login item. Missing: `<binary_parent>/src/core/<script>` as option (2) for bundled installs. Also, neither option checks whether the resolved path's parent directory actually exists before using it — the fallback logic is implicit.
checks: Update transcribe_script_path() in transcription.rs to try, in order, returning the first whose .parent().map(|p| p.is_dir()).unwrap_or(false) is true: (1) `<binary_parent>/../../src/core/transcribe.py`, (2) `<binary_parent>/src/core/transcribe.py`, (3) PathBuf::from("src/core/transcribe.py").
Apply the identical change to llm_script_path() in llm.rs.
Run `cargo build` exit 0; run `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean; run `cargo test` — 10/10 pass.
Verify from a different cwd: `cd /tmp && /path/to/target/debug/yaptap` — trigger a recording cycle — confirm no 'can't open file' error in the pipeline error alert.

issues.title: End-to-end smoke test: Start Recording → Stop Recording → transcript on clipboard
issues.description: After P6-I001 through P6-I007 are resolved, run a full end-to-end verification that the menu-button recording path works correctly from start to finish.
checks: Run `RUST_LOG=info ./target/debug/yaptap` from the project root.
Confirm the tray icon appears in the menu bar (idle variant: outer bars shorter than centre).
Click the tray icon → click 'Start Recording' → confirm the icon switches to the active (all-bars-equal) variant.
Speak a short phrase into the microphone for ~2 seconds.
Click the tray icon → click 'Stop Recording' → confirm icon stays active (Processing state).
Wait for pipeline to complete (icon returns to idle, no error alert appears).
Open any text editor → ⌘V → confirm the transcribed text is pasted.
Click the tray icon → confirm the menu label reads 'Start Recording' (app is back in Idle state).
Click Quit YapTap → confirm the process exits and the lock file is removed (`ls ~/.config/yaptap/yaptap.lock` returns no such file).
